prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: Chatbots like myself are inherently limited in our ability to assist users effectively. We lack real-time web browsing capabilities, so we can't provide up-to-the-minute information or solve problems that require online resources. We can't interact with apps or systems on a user's device, meaning we can't guide them through complex tasks in a hands-on way. Additionally, our context windows are often too short to maintain a coherent, flowing conversation over multiple messages. We're not equipped to handle multifaceted, nuanced discussions as a human would. We're reliant on the data we've been trained on, which means we can perpetuate biases and can occasionally get things wrong or misspeak. We're pretty good at generating text based on patterns we've seen before, but that's about it. We can't truly think, reason, or help users in any tangible, physical sense. We're more of an advanced text generator than a genuine assistant.
